title: haplophyllum dichotomum
intro: Haplophyllum dichotomum, also known as the forked fruited haplophyllum or forked-fruited dodder-laurel, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Haplophyllum dichotomum include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as having a potential anti-cancer effect. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or ointments, but more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
